fields.name: Dictée
fields.description: Listen and write what you hear

fields.name: Nasales
fields.description: Sons nasaux: on, an, in, un

fields.name: OI / UI
fields.description: Sons OI et UI: moi, nuit, oui

fields.french_text: Où est la gare, s'il vous plaît ?
fields.ipa: /u ɛ la ɡaʁ sil vu plɛ/
fields.english_text: Where is the train station, please?

fields.french_text: Je voudrais un café, s'il vous plaît.
fields.ipa: /ʒə vudʁɛ œ̃ kafe sil vu plɛ/
fields.english_text: I would like a coffee, please.
What is the weather like today?

fields.french_text: À quelle heure part le train ?
fields.english_text: What time does the train leave?
Do you have a free room?
Good luck and keep it up!

fields.name: en/an
fields.description: The nasal A — does not exist in English

fields.name: un/in
fields.description: Nasal vowels — like English but through nose

fields.name: ou
fields.description: Pure U sound — different from English "ou"

fields.name: eu/œu
fields.description: No equivalent in English or Hebrew

fields.name: r
fields.description: Uvular R — from the back of the throat

fields.name: é/è
fields.description: Two different E sounds in French
